turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Hey Sarah, I was looking at our CRM system the other day. You know how we've been complaining about the slow reports and all the glitches? I found this other tool, Zoho CRM. It's way cheaper, integrates better with our accounting software, and the reviews say it's faster. Should we switch?

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Oh yeah, Mike? I've heard of Zoho. Let me think... We've been on Salesforce for like four years now, right? Um, what makes you say it's better?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Well, for starters, Salesforce costs us $15k a year per team, and Zoho is half that. Plus, their dashboard is intuitive—no more digging through menus. I even did a quick trial; reports generate in seconds, not minutes. And get this, it has built-in AI for lead scoring that Salesforce charges extra for.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Hmm, sounds good on paper. But... have you thought about the migration? Remember when we switched email providers two years ago? It took weeks, and half our data got messed up. Salesforce has all our customer history baked in.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: True, migration could be a pain, but Zoho has import tools and they offer free support for it. I saw case studies where companies our size switched in a weekend. And long-term, we'd save so much money.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: A weekend? Optimistic much? Look, Salesforce isn't perfect, but our sales team knows it inside out. Training everyone on a new system? That's downtime, lost deals. We just got everyone certified last quarter—$10k down the drain if we bail now.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Okay, fair point on training. But the productivity gains... Zoho's mobile app is killer for our field reps. They complain Salesforce crashes on their phones half the time. And hey, tangent—did you see the game last night? Epic comeback.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Haha, yeah, total nail-biter. Anyway, back to this. Savings are nice, but switching costs aren't just money. Reconfiguring all our workflows, custom fields, Zapier integrations... that's dev time we don't have. Our CTO would flip.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: I get it, but the evidence is overwhelming. Gartner rates Zoho higher for mid-sized businesses like us. And Forrester says it outperforms on usability. We're leaving money on the table sticking with Salesforce.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Gartner, huh? They rate everything. Listen, Mike, I've crunched some numbers mentally here. Migration consultants alone would cost $20k, plus three months of dual-running systems. Training? Another $15k. By year two, maybe we break even—if nothing goes wrong. And it always does.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Ugh, when you put it like that... But what if we pilot it with just the sales team? Low risk.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Pilot means split data, confusion, support tickets exploding. No thanks. We've invested too much in Salesforce—custom apps, API limits we've optimized around. The sunk cost is real, and switching would reset all that. Better to negotiate a better Salesforce deal or add-ons.

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Sunk cost fallacy, Sarah! That's imperfect reasoning. The data shows Zoho is superior.

turns.speaker: B
turns.raw_text: Call it what you want, but it's reality. Those switching costs override the 'superiority' until we have bandwidth. Let's stick with Salesforce and fix the glitches with their premium support. Deal?

turns.speaker: A
turns.raw_text: Fine, you win this round. But I'm bookmarking Zoho for next budget cycle.

input.objective: Debating whether to switch from Salesforce to Zoho CRM, citing switching costs to stick with the status quo despite evidence for the alternative
